title: std::thread::scope for async-from-sync constructors: defend against unsafe transmute
description: build_client() and build_ssm_client() use std::thread::scope to spawn a thread with its own single-threaded tokio runtime. The scope join guarantee means the thread is done before the scope exits, so borrowed references (&str, etc.) are valid for the thread's lifetime without any unsafe lifetime transmutation. A contributor might suggest 'just use block_in_place or tokio::task::block_in_place'. That would panic if called from a current_thread runtime. The scope pattern works in all runtime configurations. Do not simplify this.
